records.ety: [For older tinct, fr. L. tinctus, p. p. of tingere to dye: cf. F. teinte, teint, It. tinta, tinto. See Tinge, and cf. Taint to stain, a stain, Tent a kind of wine, Tinto.]

defs: A pale or faint tinge of any color.
A color considered with reference to other very similar colors; as, red and blue are different colors, but two shades of scarlet are different tints.
A shaded effect produced by the juxtaposition of many fine parallel lines.
quotes: Or blend in beauteous tints the colored mass.
Their vigor sickens, and their tints decline.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Tinted; p. pr. & vb. n. Tinting.]

defs: To give a slight coloring to; to tinge.
